title: Scrub a secret from git history
description: A procedure to remove a committed secret from all of history after rotation, recognizing that history rewriting is disruptive.
body: Use after a committed secret has already been rotated and must be purged from history. Do not scrub before rotating, since the old value is already compromised. Steps: confirm rotation is complete; use a history-rewriting tool to remove the value across all commits; force-update the remote with coordination; have collaborators re-clone or reset; rescan history. Validation signals include a full-history scan reporting the secret gone. Risk boundary: rewriting shared history is disruptive and the secret is still compromised, so rotation is the real fix and the scrub requires team coordination and human sign-off.
